accurate.why: All claims match the bill text; description of the three tracts and trust terms is accurate.

no_hallucinations.why: No fabricated claims; 1865 treaty, tract descriptions, and gaming prohibition are all in the bill.

states_purpose.why: Clearly states the purpose is taking lands into trust for the Winnebago Tribe and making them part of the reservation.

changes_to_existing_law.why: Describes how lands are declared part of the reservation and held in trust under 1865 treaty terms.

exceptions_conditions.why: Notes the gaming prohibition, existing rights condition, and Indian lands carve-out is not applicable here.

major_provisions_covered.why: Covers all three tracts, reservation declaration, trust holding, and gaming prohibition.

concise.why: Overly detailed recitation of specific court descriptions adds unnecessary length.

correct_entities.why: All entities (Winnebago Tribe, Indian Gaming Regulatory Act) correctly named.

correct_figures.why: 1865 treaty and July 12, 2024 approval date are both accurate.

judged_via: claude-sonnet-4-6 via parallel Claude Code subagents (web/knowledge-verified context)
